title: Shri Guruvar Vrat Katha
intro: Read Shri Guruvar Vrat Katha in English and seek the blessings of Lord Vishnu and Brihaspati Dev for prosperity and good fortune.
description:
Shri Guruvar Vrat Katha

Long ago, there lived a wealthy merchant with his wife and family in a prosperous town.
They had immense wealth, comfort, and every material luxury.

However, the merchant's wife had no interest in charity or religious activities.
She never worshipped God or helped those in need.

Whenever a sage, Brahmin, or poor person came to her doorstep.
She would send them away empty-handed.

One day, Lord Brihaspati appeared at her house disguised as an old Brahmin.
He humbly asked her for alms.

The woman replied harshly,
"I have nothing to give you. Please leave my house."

The old Brahmin smiled and said,
"If you truly wish to keep your home prosperous, I can give you some advice."

Curious, the woman asked him for the remedy.
The old Brahmin deliberately gave her misleading instructions to test her wisdom and faith.

He told her to clean the house every Thursday and throw away all the useful grain.
He also advised her to wash her hair, cut her nails, and disrespect the banana tree on Thursdays.

After giving this advice, the old Brahmin quietly left.
In reality, he was Lord Brihaspati Himself.

Without thinking, the woman began following those instructions every Thursday.
Within a short time, her family's wealth and prosperity started disappearing.

The merchant suffered heavy business losses.
Soon the family struggled even to obtain food.

Their once prosperous home became filled with poverty and sorrow.
Everyone in the family became deeply worried.

After some time, the old Brahmin visited the house again.
He saw that the family had become poor and miserable.

He asked the woman,
"What has happened to your once prosperous home?"

The woman burst into tears.
She explained that she had faithfully followed everything he had instructed.

The old Brahmin smiled kindly and revealed the truth.
He told her that he had only been testing her understanding and devotion.

He explained that washing hair, cutting nails, throwing away food, and disrespecting the banana tree on Thursdays are considered inauspicious.
Instead, Thursdays should be dedicated to devotion, charity, and the worship of Lord Vishnu and Lord Brihaspati.

He advised her to observe the Guruvar Vrat with complete faith and discipline.
He explained the proper method of worship in detail.

He asked her to wear yellow clothes.
He also advised her to offer yellow flowers, Bengal gram, jaggery, turmeric, and bananas during the worship.

He emphasized the importance of worshipping the banana tree.
He also instructed her to donate yellow-colored items to the poor and listen to the Guruvar Vrat Katha.

The woman realized her mistake and sincerely repented.
She prayed for forgiveness and resolved to observe the Guruvar Vrat from the very next Thursday.

She bathed early in the morning and wore clean yellow clothes.
She worshipped Lord Vishnu and Lord Brihaspati with great devotion.

She offered Bengal gram, jaggery, yellow flowers, bananas, and turmeric.
She also worshipped the sacred banana tree according to tradition.

After listening to the Guruvar Vrat Katha.
She prayed for the happiness and prosperity of her family.

Upon completing the vrat, she donated food, yellow clothes, turmeric, and Bengal gram to the poor and Brahmins.
She served everyone with humility and devotion.

Gradually, positive changes began to appear in her life.
Her husband's business flourished once again.

Happiness, peace, and prosperity returned to their home.
Love and harmony also grew among the family members.

The woman understood that true prosperity comes through faith, charity, and righteous living.
She continued observing the Guruvar Vrat every Thursday with sincere devotion.

One day, a neighbor asked the reason behind her family's renewed happiness.
She explained the greatness and benefits of the Guruvar Vrat.

Soon the glory of the vrat spread throughout the town.
Many people began observing it with faith and devotion.

By the blessings of Lord Brihaspati and Lord Vishnu.
Many families were freed from difficulties and poverty.

People became more generous toward the poor and devoted themselves to righteous deeds.
The blessings of Lord Brihaspati filled their homes with prosperity and peace.

Since then, it is believed that anyone who observes the Guruvar Vrat with sincere faith and devotion.
Receives the blessings of Lord Vishnu and Lord Brihaspati for happiness, prosperity, wisdom, family well-being, and success in life.

faq.q1: Which deity is worshipped during the Guruvar Vrat?
faq.a1: Guruvar Vrat is dedicated to Lord Vishnu and Lord Brihaspati (the divine teacher of the gods). Devotees worship them to seek wisdom, prosperity, and family well-being.
faq.q2: When should the Guruvar Vrat Katha be recited?
faq.a2: The Guruvar Vrat Katha is traditionally recited after completing the worship of Lord Vishnu and Lord Brihaspati on Thursday.
faq.q3: What are the benefits of observing the Guruvar Vrat?
faq.a3: It is believed that observing the Guruvar Vrat brings prosperity, financial stability, marital happiness, children’s well-being, wisdom, and the blessings of Lord Vishnu and Lord Brihaspati.
faq.q4: What items are offered during Guruvar Vrat Puja?
faq.a4: Devotees commonly offer yellow clothes, turmeric, Bengal gram (chana dal), jaggery, bananas, yellow flowers, and worship the banana tree.
faq.q5: Is listening to the Guruvar Vrat Katha necessary?
faq.a5: While not mandatory, listening to or reciting the Guruvar Vrat Katha is considered an important and auspicious part of completing the Thursday fast.
faq.q6: Who can observe the Guruvar Vrat?
faq.a6: Anyone with faith and devotion can observe the Guruvar Vrat according to their tradition, health, and spiritual practice.
